name: Italy
intro: Italy exempts investment gold from VAT, but applies a stricter declaration regime and taxes the gain as miscellaneous income absent a managed savings regime.
investmentGoldText: Investment gold is the same defined category used across the EU: bars and ingots of at least 995‰ purity produced by an accredited refiner, and coins that are at least 900‰ pure, minted after 1800, are or have been legal tender in their country of origin, and are normally sold at no more than 80% above the value of the gold they contain. Italian VAT law exempts this category on the same basis as the rest of the bloc. Only gold that fits this definition qualifies for the VAT exemption and the treatment described below; jewelry and coins outside the recognized list are taxed as ordinary goods.
vatText: Investment gold is VAT-exempt on import into Italy, including from Switzerland, under the same EU directive applied across the rest of the bloc.
customsIntro: Italy applies a dual declaration requirement specific to gold, on top of the standard European €10,000 rule.

customsRows.requirement: Separate declaration to the Financial Intelligence Unit (UIF), regardless of transfer method

resaleIntro: In Italy, the resale gain is taxed as miscellaneous income at progressive rates by default. Selling through a managed savings account with an authorized intermediary can change how that tax is collected.

resaleRows.scenario: Standard sale, no managed savings regime
resaleRows.treatment: Gain taxed as miscellaneous income (redditi diversi) at progressive rates

resaleRows.scenario: Sale through a managed savings regime (risparmio amministrato/gestito)
resaleRows.treatment: The intermediary applies a substitute tax on your behalf, generally simpler and potentially more favorable than progressive rates
No minimum holding period applies; using a certified dealer simplifies reporting either way

faqs.q: Do I pay VAT on investment gold in Italy?

faqs.a: Generally as miscellaneous income at progressive rates, unless the transaction goes through a managed savings regime with an authorized intermediary.

faqs.q: Is there a minimum holding period?
faqs.a: No, Italy does not impose a minimum holding period for gold to qualify for any particular tax treatment.
